content:
# Leviticus 27 — Disagreement Log

**Chapter:** Leviticus 27 (34 verses) — Final chapter of Leviticus
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA
**Sources:** EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Total Disagreements:** 54

---

## Disagreement Index

| # | Verse | Issue |
|---|-------|-------|
| 1 | 27:1 | "Moshe" vs. "Moses" — name form |
| 2 | 27:2 | "special vow" vs. "extraordinary vow" |
| 3 | 27:2 | Sentence structure: "according to your valuation of persons" vs. "the persons shall be yours by valuation" vs. PNEUMA's restructuring |
| 4 | 27:2–7 | PNEUMA collapses the valuation table into a single prose block |
| 5 | 27:3 | "sheqels" vs. "shekels" — transliteration form |
| 6 | 27:3 | "by the shekel of the sanctuary" vs. "according to the shekel of the sanctuary" vs. "by the sanctuary standard" |
| 7 | 27:4 | "And if she is a female" (EMET) vs. "And if it is a female" (LOGOS) vs. PNEUMA paragraph format |
| 8 | 27:5 | "And if from five years old" (EMET) vs. "If the person is from five years old" (LOGOS) vs. PNEUMA table style |
| 9 | 27:6 | "for a female your valuation shall be" vs. "for a female infant" (PNEUMA) |
| 10 | 27:7 | "and upward" vs. "or over" vs. "and above" |
| 11 | 27:8 | "too poor for your valuation" vs. "too poor to pay your valuation" vs. "cannot afford the set valuation" |
| 12 | 27:8 | "he shall stand before" vs. "he shall be made to stand before" vs. "he is to bring the one being dedicated before" |
| 13 | 27:8 | "according to what the one who vowed can afford" vs. "is able to afford" (LOGOS) |
| 14 | 27:8 | PNEUMA adds paraphrase sentence: "The priest adjusts the price to what the person can pay" |
| 15 | 27:9 | "from the animals from which one brings" vs. "an animal that may be offered" vs. "an animal of the kind that may be offered" |
| 16 | 27:9 | "all that he gives from it to the LORD shall be holy" vs. "all such that any man gives to the LORD is holy" vs. "any such animal given to the LORD becomes holy" |
| 17 | 27:10 | "exchange it or swap it" (EMET) vs. "exchange it or substitute for it" (LOGOS) vs. "swap it or exchange it" (PNEUMA, reversed order) |
| 18 | 27:10 | "if he does indeed swap" (EMET) vs. "if he does in any way substitute one animal for another" (LOGOS) vs. "If they do exchange" (PNEUMA — plural subject) |
| 19 | 27:10 | "it and its substitute shall both be holy" vs. "both it and the substitute shall be holy" (LOGOS) vs. "both the original and its substitute become holy" (PNEUMA) |
| 20 | 27:11 | "of which one does not bring as an offering" (EMET) vs. "that may not be offered as an offering" (LOGOS) vs. "one that cannot be offered" (PNEUMA) |
| 21 | 27:12 | "whether it is good or bad" vs. "as either good or bad" (LOGOS) vs. "high or low" (PNEUMA) |
| 22 | 27:12 | "according to your valuation, O priest" (EMET vocative) vs. "as the priest values it, so it shall be" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) |
| 23 | 27:13 | "But if he redeems it" vs. "if he wishes to redeem it" (LOGOS) vs. "If the owner wants to buy it back" (PNEUMA) |
| 24 | 27:14 | "consecrates his house as holy" (EMET) vs. "dedicates his house as a holy thing" (LOGOS) vs. "dedicates their house as holy" (PNEUMA — plural pronoun) |
| 25 | 27:14 | "whether it is good or bad" vs. "as either good or bad" (LOGOS) vs. "high or low" (PNEUMA) — recurrence |
| 26 | 27:15 | "the one who consecrated it" (EMET) vs. "the man who dedicates it" (LOGOS adds "man") vs. "the one who dedicated it" (PNEUMA) |
| 27 | 27:15 | "a fifth of the silver of your valuation" (EMET) vs. "a fifth to the valuation price" (LOGOS) vs. "a fifth to the assessed price" (PNEUMA) |
| 28 | 27:15 | "it shall belong to him" (EMET) vs. "it shall be his" (LOGOS) vs. "it will be his again" (PNEUMA adds "again") |
| 29 | 27:16 | "some of the field of his holding" (EMET) vs. "part of the land that is his possession" (LOGOS — "land" for sedeh) vs. "a portion of his inherited land" (PNEUMA — "inherited") |
| 30 | 27:16 | "according to its seed" (EMET) vs. "in proportion to its seed" (LOGOS) vs. PNEUMA's explanatory expansion |
| 31 | 27:17 | "from the year of the jubilee" vs. "from the year of Jubilee" (LOGOS — no article) vs. "during the Year of Jubilee" (PNEUMA — temporal preposition change) |
| 32 | 27:17 | "your valuation shall stand" (EMET) vs. "it shall stand at your full valuation" (LOGOS adds "full") vs. "the full assessed value stands" (PNEUMA) |
| 33 | 27:18 | "the priest shall calculate for him the silver" (EMET) vs. "the priest shall calculate the price" (LOGOS drops "silver") vs. PNEUMA's explanatory phrasing |
| 34 | 27:18 | "it shall be deducted from your valuation" (EMET) vs. "the valuation shall be reduced" (LOGOS) vs. "reduce the assessed value accordingly" (PNEUMA) |
| 35 | 27:19 | "And if the one who dedicated the field redeems it" vs. "if he who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it" (LOGOS — desiderative) vs. "If the one who dedicated it wants to buy it back" (PNEUMA) |
| 36 | 27:19 | "it shall revert to him" (EMET/PNEUMA) vs. "it shall remain his" (LOGOS) |
| 37 | 27:21 | "when it goes out in the Jubilee" (EMET) vs. "when it is released in the Jubilee" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) |
| 38 | 27:21 | "as a devoted field" (EMET) vs. "like a field that has been devoted" (LOGOS) vs. "like a field that has been devoted and put under the ban" (PNEUMA adds gloss) |
| 39 | 27:21 | "it becomes the priest's holding" (EMET) vs. "The possession thereof shall be the priest's" (LOGOS) vs. "It passes into the priest's possession" (PNEUMA) |
| 40 | 27:22 | "which is not from the field of his holding" (EMET) vs. "which is not a part of the field of his possession" (LOGOS) vs. "rather than inherited" (PNEUMA collapses distinction) |
| 41 | 27:23 | "and he shall give your valuation on that day" (EMET) vs. "the man shall give the valuation on that day" (LOGOS) vs. "the person pays that amount on the spot" (PNEUMA — "on the spot" for ba-yom ha-hu) |
| 42 | 27:23 | "it is holy to the LORD" (EMET) vs. "as a holy thing to the LORD" (LOGOS) vs. "as something holy to the LORD" (PNEUMA) |
| 43 | 27:24 | "to the one from whom he bought it" (EMET — active) vs. "to him from whom it was bought" (LOGOS — passive) |
| 44 | 27:24 | "to him whose possession is the land" (EMET/adjusted) vs. "to whom the land belongs as a possession" (LOGOS) vs. "to the original owner of the land" (PNEUMA adds "original") |
| 45 | 27:26 | "which is made firstborn to the LORD" (EMET) vs. "which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD" (LOGOS) vs. PNEUMA's restructuring |
| 46 | 27:26 | "no man shall consecrate it" (EMET) vs. "no man may dedicate it" (LOGOS) vs. "No one may dedicate" (PNEUMA — "no one" for ish) |
| 47 | 27:26 | "whether cattle or sheep" (EMET) vs. "whether ox or sheep" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) |
| 48 | 27:28 | "any devoted thing" (EMET) vs. "no devoted thing" (LOGOS restructures negative) |
| 49 | 27:28 | "from human and animal" (EMET) vs. "whether man or animal" (LOGOS) vs. "whether a human being, an animal" (PNEUMA) |
| 50 | 27:28 | "and from the field of his possession" (EMET) vs. "or of his possession in the land" (LOGOS drops sedeh) vs. "or inherited land" (PNEUMA) |
| 51 | 27:29 | "shall be redeemed" (EMET/consensus) vs. "shall be ransomed" (LOGOS) vs. "may be ransomed" (PNEUMA — modal weakening) |
| 52 | 27:29 | "he shall surely be put to death" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "He must be put to death" (PNEUMA — drops "surely") |
| 53 | 27:30 | "from the seed of the land, from the fruit of the tree" (EMET — singular "tree") vs. "of the fruit of the trees" (LOGOS/PNEUMA — plural) |
| 54 | 27:34 | "commanded" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "gave to" (PNEUMA — weakens tsivvah); "sons of Israel" vs. "people of Israel" (PNEUMA) |

---

## Detailed Rulings

### Disagreement 1 — V. 27:1: "Moshe" vs. "Moses"
- **EMET:** "spoke to Moshe"
- **LOGOS:** "spoke to Moses"
- **PNEUMA:** "spoke to Moses"
- **Ruling:** "Moses" — English name form per project convention throughout the Leviticus series.

---

### Disagreement 2 — V. 27:2: "special vow" vs. "extraordinary vow"
- **EMET:** "makes a special vow"
- **LOGOS:** "makes a special vow"
- **PNEUMA:** "makes an extraordinary vow"
- **Hebrew:** ki yafli neder — Hifil of p-l-', "to do something extraordinary/wonderful/specific"
- **Ruling:** "makes a special vow" — EMET/LOGOS. "Special" has the stronger formal-equivalence tradition (NASB, NIV margin). "Extraordinary" (PNEUMA) is etymologically precise but less established. EMET and LOGOS agree, which is a double majority.

---

### Disagreement 3 — V. 27:2: "according to your valuation of persons to the LORD" vs. "the persons shall be yours by valuation to the LORD"
- **EMET:** "according to your valuation of persons to the LORD"
- **LOGOS:** "the persons shall be yours by valuation to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "dedicating a person to the LORD, the valuation shall be set according to the following scale" (restructured)
- **Hebrew:** be-erkekha nefashot la-YHWH — "by your valuation of persons/souls to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "according to your valuation of persons to the LORD" — EMET. The erkekha nefashot construction specifies that what follows (the table) represents the valuation system for dedicated persons. LOGOS's "the persons shall be yours by valuation" introduces "yours" as a possessive for Moses that is not standard; the MT's be-erkekha addresses Moses as the valuation-standard setter, not as an owner. PNEUMA restructures the verse entirely.

---

### Disagreement 4 — Vv. 27:2–7: PNEUMA's structural collapse of the valuation table
- **EMET:** Verse-by-verse structure maintained
- **LOGOS:** Verse-by-verse structure maintained
- **PNEUMA:** Combines the introduction (v. 2) with the entire table (vv. 3–7) into a single paragraph
- **Ruling:** Verse-by-verse structure maintained — EMET/LOGOS. Each verse in the MT is a separate legal provision and is rendered separately in the consensus. PNEUMA's table-compression is appropriate for a dynamic register but not for the consensus text.

---

### Disagreement 5 — Throughout: "sheqels" vs. "shekels"
- **EMET:** "sheqels" (Hebrew-close transliteration)
- **LOGOS:** "shekels"
- **PNEUMA:** "shekels"
- **Ruling:** "shekels" — LOGOS/PNEUMA. Standard English form. The project's consensus texts use the established English form for currency/weight terms.

---

### Disagreement 6 — V. 27:3: "by the shekel of the sanctuary" vs. "according to the shekel of the sanctuary" vs. "by the sanctuary standard"
- **EMET:** "by the sheqel of the sanctuary"
- **LOGOS:** "according to the shekel of the sanctuary"
- **PNEUMA:** "by the sanctuary standard"
- **Hebrew:** be-sheqel ha-qodesh — "by the shekel of the sanctuary/the holy shekel"
- **Ruling:** "by the shekel of the sanctuary" — EMET. "By" renders be- more directly than "according to" (LOGOS). "The sanctuary standard" (PNEUMA) drops ha-sheqel (the shekel) as the unit, which should be preserved.

---

### Disagreement 7 — V. 27:4: "And if she is a female" vs. "And if it is a female"
- **EMET:** "And if she is a female" (hi = she)
- **LOGOS:** "If it is a female"
- **PNEUMA:** Table-format, no separate verse
- **Hebrew:** ve-im neqevah hi — "and if female she"
- **Ruling:** "And if it is a female" — LOGOS. In the legal-taxonomic context, "it is a female" (the category) is cleaner than "she is a female" (which implies a specific individual). The vav-conjunction is preserved.

---

### Disagreement 8 — V. 27:5: Subject of valuation statement
- **EMET:** "And if from five years old and up to twenty years old, your valuation for a male shall be twenty shekels"
- **LOGOS:** "If the person is from five years old up to twenty years old, your valuation shall be for a male twenty shekels"
- **PNEUMA:** "For a male from five to twenty years old, twenty shekels" (table style)
- **Ruling:** "And if the person is from five years old up to twenty years old, your valuation for a male shall be twenty shekels and for a female ten shekels." LOGOS's "the person is" provides a clearer subject. EMET's vav-conjunction is preserved. PNEUMA's telegraphic style is not adopted.

---

### Disagreement 9 — V. 27:6: "for a female infant" (PNEUMA adds "infant")
- **EMET:** "for a female your valuation shall be three shekels of silver"
- **LOGOS:** "for a female your valuation shall be three shekels of silver"
- **PNEUMA:** "for a female infant, three shekels"
- **Ruling:** "for a female your valuation shall be three shekels of silver" — EMET/LOGOS. "Infant" is an interpretive addition not in the MT; even in the one-month-to-five-years age bracket, the MT does not specify "infant."

---

### Disagreement 10 — V. 27:7: "and upward" vs. "or over" vs. "and above"
- **EMET:** "and upward" (va-ma'lah)
- **LOGOS:** "or over"
- **PNEUMA:** "and above"
- **Hebrew:** va-ma'lah — "and upward" (directional adverb)
- **Ruling:** "and upward" — EMET. The MT's directional idiom va-ma'lah ("and upward") is best preserved as "and upward." "Or over" (LOGOS) introduces an alternative-disjunction (or) not in the MT. "And above" (PNEUMA) is close but less established.

---

### Disagreement 11 — V. 27:8: "too poor for your valuation" vs. "too poor to pay your valuation" vs. "cannot afford the set valuation"
- **EMET:** "too poor for your valuation" (makh hu me-erkekha)
- **LOGOS:** "too poor to pay your valuation"
- **PNEUMA:** "cannot afford the set valuation"
- **Hebrew:** ve-im makh hu me-erkekha — "and if he is low/poor from your valuation"
- **Ruling:** "too poor for your valuation" — EMET. "Too poor for" precisely renders makh hu me-erkekha (falling below the standard). "To pay" (LOGOS) adds an infinitive; "the set valuation" (PNEUMA) adds "set" beyond the MT.

---

### Disagreement 12 — V. 27:8: "he shall stand before" vs. "he shall be made to stand before" vs. "he is to bring the one being dedicated before"
- **EMET:** "he shall stand before the priest" [from ve-he'emido = Hifil, "cause to stand"]
- **LOGOS:** "he shall be made to stand before the priest"
- **PNEUMA:** "he is to bring the one being dedicated before the priest"
- **Hebrew:** ve-he'emido lifnei ha-kohen — Hifil of 'md: "he shall cause him to stand / present him before the priest"
- **Ruling:** "he shall be presented before the priest." The Hifil he'emido (causative) is more accurately rendered as "be presented" or "be made to stand" than the simple intransitive "stand" (EMET). LOGOS's "be made to stand" is accurate but slightly awkward. PNEUMA restructures around a different subject entirely.

---

### Disagreement 13 — V. 27:8: "can afford" vs. "is able to afford"
- **EMET:** "can afford"
- **LOGOS:** "is able to afford"
- **PNEUMA:** "can afford"
- **Ruling:** "can afford" — EMET/PNEUMA. "Is able to afford" (LOGOS) is verbose. "Can afford" is the natural English idiom for tassig yad ("the hand reaches").

---

### Disagreement 14 — V. 27:8: PNEUMA's added paraphrase sentence
- **PNEUMA only:** "The priest adjusts the price to what the person can pay."
- **EMET/LOGOS:** Repeat "the priest shall value him" per the MT
- **Ruling:** The MT concludes the verse with a repeated valuation statement (ya'erekhennu ha-kohen — "the priest shall value him"). This repetition is present in EMET and LOGOS and is preserved in the consensus. PNEUMA's paraphrase sentence is an addition without MT basis.

---

### Disagreement 15 — V. 27:9: "from the animals from which one brings an offering" vs. "an animal that may be offered as an offering" vs. "an animal of the kind that may be offered"
- **EMET:** "from the animals from which one brings an offering to the LORD"
- **LOGOS:** "an animal that may be offered as an offering to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "an animal of the kind that may be offered to the LORD"
- **Hebrew:** behemah asher yaqrivu mimmenah qorban la-YHWH — "animals from which they bring a qorban to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "from the animals from which one may bring an offering to the LORD." The MT specifies the category with mimmenah ("from it/from that type"), which LOGOS drops. "Offering" renders qorban. The modal "may" is added for clarity of the Imperfect yaqrivu.

---

### Disagreement 16 — V. 27:9: "all that he gives from it to the LORD shall be holy" vs. "all such that any man gives to the LORD is holy" vs. "any such animal given to the LORD becomes holy"
- **EMET:** "all that he gives from it to the LORD shall be holy"
- **LOGOS:** "all such that any man gives to the LORD is holy"
- **PNEUMA:** "any such animal given to the LORD becomes holy"
- **Hebrew:** kol asher yitten mimmenah la-YHWH yihyeh-qodesh — "all that he gives from it to the LORD shall be holy"
- **Ruling:** "all that he gives from it to the LORD shall be holy" — EMET. Mimmenah ("from it") is preserved. LOGOS adds "any man" where the MT has a simple third-person singular; PNEUMA specifies "animal" and uses "becomes" for yihyeh-qodesh.

---

### Disagreement 17 — V. 27:10: Verb order and word choice for the two exchange-verbs
- **EMET:** "He shall not exchange it [chalif] or swap it [mir]"
- **LOGOS:** "He shall not exchange it or substitute for it"
- **PNEUMA:** "The person must not swap it or exchange it" (reversed order)
- **Hebrew:** lo yachalifennu ve-lo yamir oto — two distinct verbs: chalif ("exchange") + mir ("substitute/exchange")
- **Ruling:** "He shall not exchange it or substitute for it" — LOGOS. The order chalif then mir follows the MT. "Substitute" (LOGOS) for the second verb yamir is more precise than "swap" (EMET/PNEUMA). PNEUMA reverses the verb order without textual basis.

---

### Disagreement 18 — V. 27:10: Plural subject in PNEUMA ("If they do exchange")
- **EMET:** "if he does indeed swap" — singular
- **LOGOS:** "if he does in any way substitute one animal for another" — singular
- **PNEUMA:** "If they do exchange one animal for another" — plural
- **Hebrew:** ve-im hamor yamir — third-person masculine singular throughout
- **Ruling:** Singular maintained — EMET/LOGOS. The MT uses singular consistently in this verse. PNEUMA's plural has no MT basis. The intensifying infinitive absolute (hamor yamir) is rendered "does indeed substitute" per project convention.

---

### Disagreement 19 — V. 27:10: "it and its substitute shall both be holy" vs. "both it and the substitute" vs. "both the original and its substitute"
- **EMET:** "it and its substitute shall both be holy"
- **LOGOS:** "both it and the substitute shall be holy"
- **PNEUMA:** "both the original and its substitute become holy"
- **Hebrew:** ve-hayah-hu u-temuratoh yihyeh-qodesh — "it and its substitute shall be holy"
- **Ruling:** "it and its substitute shall both be holy" — EMET. The pronoun hu ("it") refers to the original animal; temuratoh ("its substitute") uses the possessive. LOGOS's "the substitute" drops the possessive; PNEUMA's "the original" is an interpretive addition.

---

### Disagreement 20 — V. 27:11: Rendering of the negative clause
- **EMET:** "of which one does not bring as an offering to the LORD"
- **LOGOS:** "that may not be offered as an offering to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "one that cannot be offered to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "from which one may not bring an offering to the LORD." "May not" captures the prohibition (parallel negative to v. 9). LOGOS's "as an offering" retains qorban; PNEUMA drops qorban.

---

### Disagreement 21 — Vv. 27:12 and 27:14: "whether it is good or bad" vs. "as either good or bad" vs. "high or low"
- **EMET:** "whether it is good or bad" (both vv. 12 and 14)
- **LOGOS:** "as either good or bad" (both vv. 12 and 14)
- **PNEUMA:** "high or low" (both vv. 12 and 14)
- **Hebrew:** bein tov u-vein ra' — "between good and bad" (a quality-assessment formula)
- **Ruling:** "whether it is good or bad" — EMET. The MT uses tov/ra' (good/bad), not high/low. PNEUMA's "high or low" imports the pricing idiom that would be appropriate if the MT had used gavoah/shafal but is not warranted here. LOGOS's "as either" is acceptable but less direct.

---

### Disagreement 22 — V. 27:12: "according to your valuation, O priest" (EMET vocative)
- **EMET:** "according to your valuation, O priest [ke-erkekha ha-kohen]"
- **LOGOS:** "as the priest values it, so it shall be"
- **PNEUMA:** "whatever the priest says, that is what it stands at"
- **Hebrew:** ke-erkekha ha-kohen ken yihyeh — "as your valuation, O priest, so it shall be"
- **Ruling:** "as the priest values it, so it shall be" — LOGOS. The MT's ke-erkekha ha-kohen is either a vocative ("your valuation, O priest") or a third-person reference ("as the priest's valuation"). The vocative reading (EMET) is exegetically possible but the third-person rendering (LOGOS) is more standard. PNEUMA's paraphrase drops erkekha entirely.

---

### Disagreement 23 — V. 27:13: "redeems it" vs. "wishes to redeem it" vs. "wants to buy it back"
- **EMET:** "But if he redeems it [ga'ol yig'alennu — intensifying infinitive absolute]"
- **LOGOS:** "But if he wishes to redeem it"
- **PNEUMA:** "If the owner wants to buy it back"
- **Ruling:** "But if he redeems it" — EMET. The infinitive absolute ga'ol intensifying yig'alennu does not express desire but the action itself. "Wishes to" (LOGOS) and "wants to" (PNEUMA) add desiderative nuance without textual basis. "Buy it back" (PNEUMA) introduces padah-vocabulary where the MT uses ga'al.

---

### Disagreement 24 — V. 27:14: "consecrates" (EMET) vs. "dedicates" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) and plural pronoun (PNEUMA)
- **EMET:** "a man who consecrates [yaqddish] his house as holy to the LORD"
- **LOGOS:** "a man dedicates his house as a holy thing to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "someone dedicates their house as holy to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "when a man dedicates his house as holy to the LORD" — LOGOS's "dedicates" is the consensus rendering for yaqddish throughout (cf. v. 16, 17, 18 etc.); "consecrates" (EMET) is synonymous but less consistent with the chapter's overall vocabulary. "A holy thing" (LOGOS) adds the article unnecessarily. PNEUMA's "their" for a singular subject (ish) introduces an incorrect plural.

---

### Disagreement 25 — V. 27:14: Recurrence of "whether it is good or bad" vs. "as either good or bad" vs. "high or low"
- See Disagreement 21. Same ruling applies: "whether it is good or bad."

---

### Disagreement 26 — V. 27:15: "the one who consecrated it" vs. "the man who dedicates it" vs. "the one who dedicated it"
- **EMET:** "the one who consecrated it" (ha-maqddish — Hifil participle)
- **LOGOS:** "the man who dedicates it" (adds "man")
- **PNEUMA:** "the one who dedicated it"
- **Ruling:** "the one who dedicated it" — PNEUMA/EMET's phrasing adjusted. The participle ha-maqddish is gender-neutral in its scope; LOGOS's "the man" unnecessarily restricts. "Dedicated" (PNEUMA's past tense) more accurately reflects the participle as referring to someone who already performed the act.

---

### Disagreement 27 — V. 27:15: "a fifth of the silver of your valuation" vs. "a fifth to the valuation price" vs. "a fifth to the assessed price"
- **EMET:** "a fifth of the silver of your valuation to it [chamishit kesef-erkekha alav]"
- **LOGOS:** "a fifth to the valuation price"
- **PNEUMA:** "a fifth to the assessed price"
- **Hebrew:** chamishit kesef-erkekha — "a fifth of the silver of your valuation"
- **Ruling:** "a fifth of the silver of your valuation to it" — EMET. The MT specifies kesef ("silver/money") explicitly in the compound kesef-erkekha; LOGOS and PNEUMA collapse this to "price."

---

### Disagreement 28 — V. 27:15: "it shall belong to him" vs. "it shall be his" vs. "it will be his again"
- **EMET:** "it shall belong to him"
- **LOGOS:** "it shall be his"
- **PNEUMA:** "it will be his again"
- **Hebrew:** ve-hayah lo — "and it shall be to him"
- **Ruling:** "and it shall be his" — LOGOS. "Again" (PNEUMA) adds a restoration nuance not explicit in the MT's simple ve-hayah lo.

---

### Disagreement 29 — V. 27:16: "the field of his holding/possession" — sedeh vs. "land"
- **EMET:** "some of the field of his holding [sedeh achuzato]"
- **LOGOS:** "part of the land that is his possession" (uses "land" for sedeh)
- **PNEUMA:** "a portion of his inherited land"
- **Hebrew:** mi-sedeh achuzato — "from the field of his possession"
- **Ruling:** "some of the field of his possession" — EMET. Sedeh specifically means "field" (agricultural land, not simply "land/territory" in general). LOGOS's "land" is broader than sedeh warrants. "Inherited" (PNEUMA) is interpretive of achuzah (which means inalienable holding/possession, which implies inheritance, but the word itself means possession).

---

### Disagreement 30 — V. 27:16: "according to its seed" vs. "in proportion to its seed" vs. PNEUMA's expansion
- **EMET:** "your valuation shall be according to its seed [le-fi zar'o]"
- **LOGOS:** "your valuation shall be in proportion to its seed"
- **PNEUMA:** "the value shall be set according to how much seed it takes to sow it"
- **Ruling:** "according to its seed" — EMET. Le-fi zar'o ("according to the mouth of its seed") is the technical formula. "In proportion to" (LOGOS) is accurate but adds a mathematical connotation; "according to" is more direct. PNEUMA's explanatory expansion goes beyond the MT.

---

### Disagreement 31 — V. 27:17: "from the year of the Jubilee" — article presence
- **EMET:** "from the year of the jubilee" (mi-shenat ha-yovel — with article ha-)
- **LOGOS:** "from the year of Jubilee" (no article)
- **PNEUMA:** "during the Year of Jubilee" (changes preposition)
- **Ruling:** "from the year of the Jubilee" — EMET. The MT has ha-yovel (with the definite article). Capitalizing "Jubilee" as a proper title is maintained. "During" (PNEUMA) introduces a temporal locative not present in the MT's mi- ("from").

---

### Disagreement 32 — V. 27:17: "your valuation shall stand" vs. "it shall stand at your full valuation" vs. "the full assessed value stands"
- **EMET:** "your valuation shall stand [be-erkekha yaqum]"
- **LOGOS:** "it shall stand at your full valuation"
- **PNEUMA:** "the full assessed value stands"
- **Ruling:** "your valuation shall stand" — EMET. Be-erkekha yaqum is "by your valuation it shall stand." Neither LOGOS nor PNEUMA has textual basis for "full" (LOGOS adds it to explain that the complete 49-year value applies, but the MT does not add a modifier).

---

### Disagreement 33 — V. 27:18: "the priest shall calculate for him the silver" vs. "the priest shall calculate the price"
- **EMET:** "the priest shall calculate for him the silver [et-ha-kesef]"
- **LOGOS:** "the priest shall calculate the price according to the years that remain"
- **PNEUMA:** "the priest will calculate the price proportionally, according to how many years remain"
- **Hebrew:** ve-chishav-lo ha-kohen et-ha-kesef al-pi ha-shanim ha-notirot — "the priest shall calculate for him the silver according to the years remaining"
- **Ruling:** "then the priest shall calculate for him the silver according to the years that remain until the year of the Jubilee" — EMET's "the silver" is preserved (kesef is explicit); lo ("for him") is preserved; LOGOS's structural framing is adopted. "Proportionally" (PNEUMA) is an added adverb.

---

### Disagreement 34 — V. 27:18: "it shall be deducted from your valuation" vs. "the valuation shall be reduced" vs. "reduce the assessed value accordingly"
- **EMET:** "and it shall be deducted from your valuation [ve-nigar me-erkekha — Niphal of g-r-']"
- **LOGOS:** "and the valuation shall be reduced"
- **PNEUMA:** "and reduce the assessed value accordingly"
- **Hebrew:** ve-nigar me-erkekha — "and it shall be diminished/deducted from your valuation"
- **Ruling:** "and it shall be deducted from your valuation" — EMET. Nigar (Niphal of g-r-') means "to be diminished/reduced/deducted"; "deducted" is the most precise English equivalent for a pro-rata subtraction. "Reduced" (LOGOS) is acceptable but vaguer. "Accordingly" (PNEUMA) is a filler addition.

---

### Disagreement 35 — V. 27:19: "redeems it" vs. "wishes to redeem it" vs. "wants to buy it back"
- See Disagreement 23. Same ruling applies to the parallel construction in v. 19.
- **EMET:** "And if the one who dedicated the field redeems it [ga'ol yig'al — intensifying infinitive absolute]"
- **LOGOS:** "And if he who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it"
- **PNEUMA:** "If the one who dedicated it wants to buy it back"
- **Ruling:** "And if the one who dedicated the field redeems it" — EMET.

---

### Disagreement 36 — V. 27:19: "it shall revert to him" vs. "it shall remain his"
- **EMET:** "it shall revert to him [ve-qam lo — Qal of q-w-m]"
- **LOGOS:** "it shall remain his"
- **PNEUMA:** "the field reverts to him"
- **Hebrew:** ve-qam lo — "and it shall stand/revert to him"
- **Ruling:** "it shall revert to him" — EMET/PNEUMA. "Remain his" (LOGOS) implies continuity of ownership, but the field has been consecrated — it is returning after dedication. "Revert" correctly expresses the restoration after a break. PNEUMA's "reverts" is equivalent; "it shall revert" follows the future-indicative register of the chapter.

---

### Disagreement 37 — V. 27:21: "when it goes out in the Jubilee" vs. "when it is released in the Jubilee"
- **EMET:** "when it goes out in the jubilee [be-tsetoh va-yovel]"
- **LOGOS:** "when it is released in the Jubilee"
- **PNEUMA:** "when that field is released in the Jubilee"
- **Hebrew:** be-tsetoh va-yovel — "in its going out / at its going-forth in the Jubilee" (Qal infinitive construct of yatsa')
- **Ruling:** "when it goes out in the Jubilee" — EMET. The root yatsa' ("to go out") is the MT's specific vocabulary for the Jubilee's release-mechanism; "goes out" preserves this. "Released" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is an interpretive rendering that loses the yatsa' connection. PNEUMA adds "that field" for clarity but this is not in the MT.

---

### Disagreement 38 — V. 27:21: "as a devoted field" vs. "like a field that has been devoted" vs. "like a field that has been devoted and put under the ban"
- **EMET:** "as a devoted field [ki-sedeh ha-cherem]"
- **LOGOS:** "like a field that has been devoted"
- **PNEUMA:** "like a field that has been devoted and put under the ban"
- **Hebrew:** ki-sedeh ha-cherem — "like a devoted-field / like a field of the cherem"
- **Ruling:** "as a devoted field" — EMET. The construct chain sedeh ha-cherem ("field of the devoted-thing") is compact. LOGOS unpacks it accurately but adds words. PNEUMA adds "and put under the ban" which anticipates vv. 28–29 but goes beyond the MT.

---

### Disagreement 39 — V. 27:21: "it becomes the priest's holding" vs. "The possession thereof shall be the priest's" vs. "It passes into the priest's possession"
- **EMET:** "it becomes the priest's holding [la-kohen tihyeh achuzato]"
- **LOGOS:** "The possession thereof shall be the priest's"
- **PNEUMA:** "It passes into the priest's possession"
- **Hebrew:** la-kohen tihyeh achuzato — "to the priest shall be his possession/his holding"
- **Ruling:** "it shall become the priest's possession." "Possession" renders achuzah (the term for inalienable land-holding) consistently with v. 16. "Thereof" (LOGOS) is archaic. "Passes into" (PNEUMA) introduces a transfer-idiom not in the MT.

---

### Disagreement 40 — V. 27:22: "which is not from the field of his holding" vs. "which is not a part of the field of his possession" vs. "rather than inherited"
- **EMET:** "which is not from the field of his holding [mi-sedeh achuzato]"
- **LOGOS:** "which is not a part of the field of his possession"
- **PNEUMA:** "rather than inherited" (collapses the distinction)
- **Hebrew:** asher lo mi-sedeh achuzato — "which is not from the field of his possession"
- **Ruling:** "which is not a part of the field of his possession" — LOGOS. The clause must explicitly distinguish purchased (miqnah) from ancestral (achuzah) land; PNEUMA's "rather than inherited" compresses this into a contrast without naming either category. LOGOS and EMET both preserve the achuzah terminology; LOGOS's "a part of" is slightly cleaner than EMET's "from."

---

### Disagreement 41 — V. 27:23: "on that day" vs. "on the spot"
- **EMET:** "on that day [ba-yom ha-hu]"
- **LOGOS:** "on that day"
- **PNEUMA:** "on the spot"
- **Ruling:** "on that day" — EMET/LOGOS. Ba-yom ha-hu is a specific temporal formula ("on that day/on that very day"). "On the spot" (PNEUMA) is colloquial and loses the formulaic character.

---

### Disagreement 42 — V. 27:23: "it is holy to the LORD" vs. "as a holy thing" vs. "as something holy"
- **EMET:** "it is holy to the LORD [qodesh la-YHWH]"
- **LOGOS:** "as a holy thing to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "as something holy to the LORD"
- **Hebrew:** qodesh la-YHWH — a predicate statement: "[it is] holy to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "it is holy to the LORD" — EMET. Qodesh la-YHWH is a predicate designation; "as a holy thing" (LOGOS) and "as something holy" (PNEUMA) both introduce "as," converting the predicate to an appositive.

---

### Disagreement 43 — V. 27:24: Active vs. passive construction
- **EMET:** "to the one from whom he bought it [la'asher qanahu me-itto]" (active: the dedicant bought it)
- **LOGOS:** "to him from whom it was bought" (passive)
- **PNEUMA:** "to the one it was bought from" (passive)
- **Hebrew:** qanahu me-itto — "he bought it from him" (active)
- **Ruling:** "to the one from whom he bought it" — EMET. The active construction preserves the dedicant as the agent of purchase, which is the MT's point: it reverts to the original owner from whom the dedicant (not some third party) purchased it.

---

### Disagreement 44 — V. 27:24: "to him whose possession is the land" vs. "to whom the land belongs as a possession" vs. "to the original owner of the land"
- **EMET:** "to him whose ancestral holding is the land [la'asher-lo achuzat ha-aretz]"
- **LOGOS:** "to whom the land belongs as a possession"
- **PNEUMA:** "to the original owner of the land"
- **Ruling:** "to him whose possession is the land" — EMET/adjusted. The achuzah terminology is preserved. "Original owner" (PNEUMA) adds "original" without MT basis. LOGOS's "as a possession" is accurate but slightly restructured.

---

### Disagreement 45 — V. 27:26: "which is made firstborn to the LORD" vs. "which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD"
- **EMET:** "which is made firstborn to the LORD [asher yevukkar la-YHWH — Pual passive]"
- **LOGOS:** "which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD"
- **PNEUMA:** "because the firstborn already belongs to the LORD" (restructured)
- **Hebrew:** asher yevukkar la-YHWH — Pual imperfect of b-k-r: "that is/shall be treated as firstborn to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "which as a firstborn belongs to the LORD" — LOGOS. The Pual expresses the firstborn's inherent status as belonging to YHWH. LOGOS's "as a firstborn belongs to" is the clearest English rendering. EMET's "is made firstborn" is technically accurate for the Pual but unusual idiom. PNEUMA restructures as a causal clause.

---

### Disagreement 46 — V. 27:26: "shall" vs. "may" for the prohibition; "no man" vs. "no one"
- **EMET:** "no man shall consecrate it [lo yaqddish ish oto]"
- **LOGOS:** "no man may dedicate it"
- **PNEUMA:** "No one may dedicate a firstborn animal to the LORD"
- **Ruling:** "no man may dedicate it" — LOGOS. Modal "may" is more appropriate for a prohibition of capability/permission. "No man" preserves ish; PNEUMA's "no one" softens the masculine term. PNEUMA also moves "a firstborn animal to the LORD" into this clause, restructuring unnecessarily.

---

### Disagreement 47 — V. 27:26: "whether cattle or sheep" vs. "whether ox or sheep"
- **EMET:** "whether cattle or sheep [im shor im seh]"
- **LOGOS:** "whether ox or sheep"
- **PNEUMA:** "Whether ox or sheep"
- **Hebrew:** shor — a single bovine animal (ox/bull/head of cattle); seh — a single sheep or goat
- **Ruling:** "whether ox or sheep" — LOGOS/PNEUMA. Shor is singular ("ox/head of cattle"); "cattle" (EMET) is a collective plural. The singular "ox" is more precise.

---

### Disagreement 48 — V. 27:28: "any devoted thing" (EMET) vs. "no devoted thing" (LOGOS restructures)
- **EMET:** "But any devoted thing [akh kol-cherem] that a man devotes to the LORD... shall not be sold and shall not be redeemed"
- **LOGOS:** "But no devoted thing that a man devotes to the LORD... shall be sold or redeemed"
- **PNEUMA:** "However, nothing that a person devotes to the LORD... may be sold or bought back"
- **Hebrew:** akh kol-cherem asher yacharem ish la-YHWH... lo yimmaker... ve-lo yig'al
- **Ruling:** "But any devoted thing that a man devotes to the LORD... shall not be sold and shall not be redeemed" — EMET. The MT's structure uses the positive noun (kol-cherem) followed by two negated verbs. LOGOS restructures to "no devoted thing...shall be sold or redeemed," combining the negations into a single clause. EMET's two separate negations (lo yimmaker / ve-lo yig'al) are formally distinct and should be preserved separately.

---

### Disagreement 49 — V. 27:28: "from human and animal" vs. "whether man or animal" vs. "whether a human being, an animal"
- **EMET:** "from human and animal [me-adam u-vehemah]"
- **LOGOS:** "whether man or animal"
- **PNEUMA:** "whether a human being, an animal"
- **Hebrew:** me-adam u-vehemah — "from man and from animal" (partitive compound)
- **Ruling:** "from human and animal" — EMET. The partitive me- ("from") is present in the MT and should be preserved. "Whether...or" (LOGOS) converts the partitive to a disjunctive.

---

### Disagreement 50 — V. 27:28: "and from the field of his possession" vs. "or of his possession in the land" vs. "or inherited land"
- **EMET:** "and from the field of his holding [u-mi-sedeh achuzato]"
- **LOGOS:** "or of his possession in the land" (drops sedeh / "field")
- **PNEUMA:** "or inherited land" (drops both sedeh and achuzah)
- **Hebrew:** u-mi-sedeh achuzato — "and from the field of his possession"
- **Ruling:** "and from the field of his possession" — EMET. Both sedeh ("field") and achuzah ("possession/holding") are present in the MT and should be rendered. LOGOS drops sedeh; PNEUMA drops both terms.

---

### Disagreement 51 — V. 27:29: "shall be redeemed" vs. "shall be ransomed" vs. "may be ransomed"
- **EMET:** "shall not be redeemed [lo yifdeh — Niphal of p-d-h]"
- **LOGOS:** "shall be ransomed" (implied negative from verse structure)
- **PNEUMA:** "may be ransomed" (modal weakening)
- **Hebrew:** lo yifdeh — "shall not be redeemed/ransomed"
- **Ruling:** "shall be redeemed" for the negative — consensus uses "shall not be redeemed" (EMET). The verb p-d-h ("to redeem/ransom") is rendered "redeemed" for consistency with the chapter's dominant ga'al-based redemption language. "Ransomed" (LOGOS) is an acceptable synonym but introduces a term not established in this chapter. "May be ransomed" (PNEUMA) weakens the absolute negation.

---

### Disagreement 52 — V. 27:29: "shall surely be put to death" vs. "must be put to death"
- **EMET:** "he shall surely be put to death [mot yumat — intensifying infinitive absolute]"
- **LOGOS:** "he shall surely be put to death"
- **PNEUMA:** "He must be put to death"
- **Hebrew:** mot yumat — Qal infinitive absolute + Hophal imperfect: "dying he shall die / he shall surely be put to death"
- **Ruling:** "he shall surely be put to death" — EMET/LOGOS. The infinitive absolute mot demands the intensifying "surely." PNEUMA's "must be" loses this intensification. EMET and LOGOS agree.

---

### Disagreement 53 — V. 27:30: "from the fruit of the tree" (singular) vs. "of the fruit of the trees" (plural)
- **EMET:** "from the fruit of the tree [mi-peri ha-etz — singular ha-etz]"
- **LOGOS:** "or of the fruit of the trees" (plural)
- **PNEUMA:** "the fruit of the trees" (plural)
- **Hebrew:** mi-peri ha-etz — "from the fruit of the tree" (singular collective ha-etz)
- **Ruling:** "from the fruit of the tree" — EMET. Ha-etz is singular (the collective "tree") in the MT; LOGOS and PNEUMA pluralize. The singular collective is the more precise rendering.

---

### Disagreement 54 — V. 27:34: "commanded" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "gave to" (PNEUMA); "sons of Israel" vs. "people of Israel" (PNEUMA)
- **EMET:** "the commandments that the LORD commanded Moshe for the sons of Yisra'el on Mount Sinai"
- **LOGOS:** "the commandments that the LORD commanded Moses for the sons of Israel on Mount Sinai"
- **PNEUMA:** "the commandments that the LORD gave to Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai"
- **Hebrew:** asher tsivvah YHWH et-Mosheh el-benei Yisrael be-har Sinai
- **Ruling (part 1 — verb):** "commanded" — EMET/LOGOS. Tsivvah (Pi'el of ts-w-h) means "to command/charge/commission" — a verb of authoritative directive. "Gave to" (PNEUMA) substitutes a neutral transfer-verb, flattening the commanding force of tsivvah.
- **Ruling (part 2 — recipients):** "sons of Israel" — EMET/LOGOS. The project's established rendering for bney yisrael. "People of Israel" (PNEUMA) is a common informal equivalent but departs from the project standard.
- **Ruling (part 3 — name):** "Moses" — English form per project convention (EMET's "Moshe" is not the consensus standard).

---

## Summary Note

Leviticus 27 produced 54 logged disagreements across 34 verses. The primary sources of divergence were:

1. **PNEUMA's structural compression** — combining the valuation table (vv. 2–7) into a single block, using telegraphic phrase-style for the tithe regulations (vv. 30–33), and adding explanatory glosses throughout;
2. **PNEUMA's modal weakening** — substituting "wishes to," "wants to," "may" for the MT's indicative or intensifying-infinitive-absolute constructions;
3. **PNEUMA's pronoun number shifts** — introducing plural "their" and "they" for MT singular subjects;
4. **LOGOS's occasional omissions** — dropping sedeh ("field") in favor of "land," dropping specific MT terms (kesef/"silver," mimmenah/"from it") in favor of more concise English;
5. **EMET/LOGOS disagreements** — primarily on verb modality ("shall" vs. "may" for prohibitions), proper names, and the rendering of technical assessment formulas.

The closing colophon (v. 34) was unanimous in substance between EMET and LOGOS and is rendered with full fidelity to the MT: "These are the commandments that the LORD commanded Moses for the sons of Israel on Mount Sinai." This sentence seals the entire book of Leviticus.

---

*SOPHIA — Leviticus 27 Disagreement Log complete. Final chapter of Leviticus. 54 disagreements logged across 34 verses.*
